response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong web, Node.js/TypeScript, and DLang systems experience, but the role demands deep, current C++ expertise for industrial automation. His C++ exposure is limited to a 7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a bachelor thesis project, with no evidence of MQTT, message brokers in C++, IEC 62443/SDL practices, or industrial-cybersecurity work. While his low-latency DLang experience shows systems aptitude, the gap between his profile and the core requirements is too large for a senior C++ role.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software experience across web, embedded, and desktop systems
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of requests/sec) using async I/O and fibers at Dunnhumby
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014-2015); C++ absent from listed skills
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or OT-protocol experience
Career trajectory centers on web/full-stack and TypeScript, misaligned with a hands-on C++ architecture role

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443 background
Proven high-performance systems and concurrency experience (DLang)
